item.title: Tether’s Brief Flip of Ether in Value Gives Crypto a Reality Check
item.description: For a few hours last weekend, Tether’s USDT stablecoin became more valuable than Ether.

item.title: Latest AI Doomers Imagine How Europe ‘Slides Into Irrelevance’
item.description: Report urges ‘most ambitious’ effort to keep pace in the AI race

item.title: ECB Stands Out With First G7 Rate Hike Since Start of Iran War
item.description: The problem with doing your own thing is that you look more exposed if you’re wrong.

item.title: Credit Traders Unwind a $20 Billion Wartime Short in Europe
item.description: Credit investors have given up their wartime hedges on European companies.

item.title: UK Power Prices Turn Negative as Renewables Set to Flood Grid
item.description: UK power prices turned negative for the first time since late April as strong wind and solar generation are set to flood the grid.

item.title: ENN Natural Gas Ditches Restructuring Plan, $12 Billion Buyout
item.description: ENN Natural Gas Co.’s board terminated a planned restructuring, including a nearly $12 billion buyout offer for ENN Energy Holdings Ltd. and a second listing in Hong Kong.

item.title: India Inflation Accelerates to 3.93%, Stays Below RBI Target
item.description: India’s inflation accelerated in May but remained below the Reserve Bank of India’s 4% target, even as higher fuel costs stemming from disruptions in global energy markets added to price pressures.

item.title: EU’s Top Carmakers Unite to Push for ‘Made in Europe’ Plans
item.description: Volkswagen AG, Stellantis NV and Renault SA are joining forces to push for regulation favoring automaking in Europe to help them compete with a surge of affordable cars from China.

item.title: SpaceX Poised to Pop After Record IPO
item.description: Shadow-market trading suggests a 35% gain for the shares.

item.title: The Next US-China AI Battle Is Over Compute — and China Is Spending Big
item.description: China bets $295 billion on AI backbone as US data center boom accelerates

item.title: Singapore Court Stays Order to Jail Byju’s Founder, Lawyer Says
item.description: Singapore’s high court has granted a stay on a six-month jail term for the founder of failed Indian technology firm Byju’s, his law firm Lazareff Le Bars said.

item.title: Banks Court Wealthy Indian Diaspora in $50 Billion Deposit Drive
item.description: After more than a decade, India is once again turning to its 35 million-strong diaspora for support. This time, banks are mounting a global campaign to attract more than $50 billion in deposits from overseas nationals as policymakers seek to bolster foreign exchange reserves and support a battered rupee.

item.title: South Africa Treasury Says Upgrades Show Path to Investment Grade
item.description: South Africa’s recent credit-rating upgrades are a strong endorsement of government policy and show the nation can restore its coveted investment-grade status if it stays on track, said the National Treasury chief.

item.title: Swiss Firms Expect Population Cap to Be Defanged, Lobby Says
item.description: Swiss companies expect the government to water down the proposed population cap if voters back it, according to the country’s main business lobby.

item.title: Do Humanoid Robots Really Need Legs?
item.description: There’s a long-running disagreement among roboticists about whether wheeled robots make more sense than walking ones.

item.title: Higher Costs for Plastics Used Everywhere Are Becoming Next Inflation Headache
item.description: US plastic suppliers say they are running out of room to absorb high costs of raw materials, raising the prospect of price increases for consumer goods ranging from groceries to cars later this year.

item.title: Economists Push Fed Rate-Cut Expectations Into 2027, Survey Shows
item.description: Economists moved back their expectation for interest-rate cuts and now see the Federal Reserve holding rates steady into the middle of next year, according to a Bloomberg News survey.

item.title: Former Investment Banker Debuts Low-Cost Video AI for India
item.description: A startup founded by a former banker has launched one of India’s first homegrown video AI platforms, wading into an arena dominated by US and Chinese services.

item.title: Deutsche Bank Is Arranging Debut SRT for Renewables Lender DKB
item.description: Deutsche Kreditbank AG, one of Germany’s largest lenders to sustainability projects, is planning its debut transaction in the fast-growing market for significant risk transfers.

item.title: Korea Pension Fund’s Pivot Amplified Stock Swings, Barclays Says
item.description: A move by the National Pension Service to temporarily suspend portfolio rebalancing has fueled volatility in South Korea’s stock market and added pressure on the won, according to Barclays Plc.

item.title: Sleep Number Files for Bankruptcy Sale to Sleep Country Canada
item.description: Mattress maker Sleep Number Corp. has agreed to merge with Sleep Country Canada through a court-supervised process after years of weak demand and mounting financial pressure.

item.title: Goldman’s Waldron Says SpaceX Shows Investor Zeal for Funding AI
item.description: SpaceX’s record-setting initial public offering shows investors are eager to finance artificial intelligence infrastructure, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. President John Waldron.

item.title: Banks Line Up €1.7 Billion of Debt for Triton’s Flender Buyout
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. are among banks that have lined up around €1.7 billion ($1.97 billion) of debt financing that will back Triton Partners’ acquisition of German gearbox-maker Flender GmbH.

item.title: China Credit Growth Tops Forecasts in Rebound From Lending Slump
item.description: China’s credit expansion held up better than expected in May, rebounding after a rare contraction in loans the previous month.

item.title: Indian Developer Prestige Plans $6.3 Billion in Project Launches
item.description: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd., which counts BlackRock Inc. and Vanguard Group Inc. among its investors, is targeting about 600 billion rupees ($6.3 billion) in estimated sales value for this financial year as the developer prepares to launch projects across the country, its billionaire founder said.

item.title: SpaceX Prepares for Debut as $75 Billion IPO Breaks Record
item.description: SpaceX made history with a $75 billion IPO that instantly turned it into one of the biggest public companies in the world. Now it has to win over the market.

item.title: Germany to Grow in 2026 as Spending Offsets War, Bundesbank Says
item.description: Germany’s economy will recover more slowly than expected because of the Iran war but pick up steam in the coming years thanks to fiscal stimulus, according to the Bundesbank.

item.title: Scammers Used Gemini AI to Help Build Spam Messages, Google Says
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google filed a lawsuit against a suspected Chinese cybercrime operation, alleging that the group used artificial intelligence to send more than 2 million text messages with fraudulent links attempting to scam cellphone users.

item.title: Britons See Inflation Hitting Double BOE Target, Poll Finds
item.description: Britons expect inflation to accelerate sharply in the coming year while businesses are turning more pessimistic, according to two Bank of England surveys that highlight the growing trade-off posed by the Iran war.

item.title: European Bonds Surge as Trump Touts Progress on Iran Peace Deal
item.description: European bonds rallied as oil prices slid on the renewed potential for a peace deal in the Middle East.
